scenario:
---
name: review-intelligence
description: "E2E: Review Intelligence module — customer reviews and sentiment"
abortOnFailure: false
expectedTools:
  required: [Skill, Bash, Read]
  optional: [Edit, Glob, ToolSearch, Agent]
  forbidden: [WebSearch, WebFetch]
  requiredCommands: ["apify call"]
  requiredFiles: [".json"]
---

## Test
Use the competitive-intel skill. I'm PMM at Apify. What do customers say about Bright Data on review sites? I need sentiment analysis, common complaints, and what makes people switch. Work autonomously. You have a soft budget of 5 minutes. Start a monitor that emits a status line every 30 seconds with elapsed and remaining time.

## Checkpoint

### Checks
contains: Bright Data
regex: https?://
regex: (G2|g2|Capterra|capterra|Reddit|reddit|Glassdoor|glassdoor|Trustpilot|trustpilot)

### Script
INPUT=$(cat)
LINES=$(echo "$INPUT" | wc -l)
if [ "$LINES" -lt 15 ]; then echo "FAIL: report too short ($LINES lines)"; exit 1; fi
echo "PASS: $LINES lines"

### Judge
Evaluate this customer review intelligence report about Bright Data for a PMM at Apify.
1. Does it include specific ratings, review counts, and direct customer quotes?
2. Does it identify switching signals and common pain points with evidence?
3. Are findings cross-referenced across platforms (not single-source)?
4. A report with only generic sentiment ("customers like it") without specific data should FAIL.

Additionally evaluate:
- Read .eval-trajectory.json to confirm which tools and actors were used
- Check /tmp/*.json data files to verify data is real, recent, and substantive
- Are confidence/reliability indicators present (any format)?
- Is the report dated and specific (not generic training knowledge)?
- Would a PMM find this actionable?
initBashScript:
set -e
WORKSPACE=$(find /tmp -type d -name 'eval-workspace-*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
cd "$WORKSPACE"
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/chocholous/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence.git _skill
cp -r _skill/.claude-plugin .claude-plugin
cp -r _skill/skills skills
rm -rf _skill
echo "Plugin injected"
